summary: Solid plan. Correct interfaces, patterns, and file paths. One step-ordering issue and minor notes below.

notes.detail: Fragment shader (step 5) must exist before ribbonField.ts (step 2) can import it. Move step 5 to step 1b or combine with step 1 so both shaders are created before the geometry system.

WaveField did not have a dedicated quality field — it used a ternary on tier in main.ts. Adding maxRibbonPoints to QualityProfile is cleaner but diverges from the prior pattern. Fine to proceed, just noting the intentional improvement.

Plan correctly identifies 3 modes (particles, ribbon, pointcloud). Existing main.ts passes maxWaves via tier ternary; new plan uses quality.maxRibbonPoints — remember to remove the old ternary logic entirely.
Tests should also verify that init adds THREE.Points (not THREE.Line or THREE.Group) to confirm the 3D requirement. Plan lists this as T-034-07 — good.

Ribbon point distribution along helical curves is the most complex new logic. Ensure PRNG seeding uses a distinct sub-seed (seed + ':ribbon') to avoid correlation with pointCloud's (seed + ':pointcloud') distribution.

interface_check.GeometrySystem: Correct — init(scene, seed, params), draw(scene, frame), cleanup?()
interface_check.FrameState: Correct — time, delta, elapsed, params, width, height, pointerX?, pointerY?
interface_check.VisualParams: Correct — all uniform source fields match
interface_check.QualityProfile: Correct — adding maxRibbonPoints is additive, non-breaking
interface_check.shader_uniforms: All 15 uniforms match existing pointWarp pattern
